title: Ludia pinnatinervia
intro: Ludia pinnatinervia, also known as the pinene-leaf ludia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Ludia pinnatinervia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ludia pinnatinervia is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be found in some commercial herbal supplements and topical creams.
taxonomy: Ludia pinnatinervia belongs to the family Gesneriaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint, basil, and honeysuckle. Ludia pinnatinervia can be further classified into the genus Ludia, which is a small genus of flowering plants.
